source_jobs.title: Vice President, Corporate Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

EducationSuperHighway, the national non-profit that connected 99.7% of American students to high-speed internet and 5 million low-income households to affordable broadband, is now launching its 3.0 mission to end the reading crisis in America’s K-12 schools.

Today, two-thirds of students cannot read at grade level by the end of third grade, when a student goes from learning to read to reading to learn. We know how to solve this problem - provide every student who enters first grade reading below grade level with a year of 1:1 literacy tutoring. The challenge has been funding tutoring for millions of students.

The federal Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC) creates a new opportunity to support literacy tutoring through tax-credit-eligible giving. EducationSuperHighway is building the partnerships needed to bring this opportunity to life.

We are looking for a mission-driven, entrepreneurial leader to help build and execute our employer partnerships strategy and lay the foundation for long-term growth.

This is EducationSuperHighway's first corporate partnerships hire. You will pioneer a playbook from scratch, on two parallel tracks: direct employer outreach and payroll processor platform partnerships.

For the next six months, you will operate as a highly independent leader: developing the pitch, building the pipeline, managing senior external relationships, and helping define what this function should become over time. As the work grows, you will help build and lead a small team.



What You Will Do

Track 1: Direct Employer Partnerships

 - Develop and execute an outbound prospecting strategy targeting employers across sectors, with strong CSR/ESG commitments and employee bases that create meaningful donation capacity

 - Own the full sales cycle from cold outreach through signed partnership agreement — pitch development, multi-stakeholder navigation, and close

 - Sell to executive leadership across HR, benefits, and CSR/ESG functions, positioning the EFTC as a transformational employee benefit and corporate impact opportunity

 - Track a pipeline of employer prospects with rigorous CRM discipline, reporting weekly against partnership goals

Track 2: Payroll & HR Platform Business Development

 - Identify and pursue partnerships with payroll, HR and related platform companies integrate EFTC donation functionality into employee portals

 - Build relationships with platform BD, product, and partnership teams to position EducationSuperHighway as a preferred SGO and giving infrastructure partner

 - Explore co-marketing and partnership structures that support long-term platform engagement and promotion

 - Treat this track as equal in priority to direct employer outreach

Playbook & Team Building

 - Document a repeatable sales process, pitch library, objection handling, and partner onboarding workflow that a growing team can execute at scale

 - Build and lead a team in preparation for the 2027 EFTC launch

 - Collaborate closely with the Employer Success Manager to ensure smooth handoffs and a high-quality partner experience


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Required

 - 7+ years of experience in B2B partnership, enterprise BD, or platform partnerships with a track record of closing complex, multi-stakeholder deals

 - Proven hunter who has built pipeline from scratch and closed deals in a first-mover, startup, or ambiguous environment

 - Experience selling to HR, People, Benefits, or CSR leaders at enterprise and/or mid-market employers

 - Exceptional executive presence. You can hold a room with a CEO or CHRO and navigate a internal sales process with multiple stakeholders

 - Highly self-directed, and comfortable building from zero

 - Experience in building and leading a sales or partnerships team

 - AI-forward and quick to leverage AI to work smarter and faster

 - Genuine passion for the mission of closing the K-12 literacy gap

Highly Valued

 - Background at a benefits consulting firm, such as Mercer, Aon

source_jobs.title: Contract Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Location: Remote

Employment type: Contract, Full-time

Compensation: Base retainer plus per-placement bonus, commensurate with experience

About EducationSuperHighway
EducationSuperHighway is a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit that closed the K-12 broadband gap, connecting 99.3% of America's public schools to high-speed internet and helping 5 million low-income households get online. Now, EducationSuperHighway is turning those connections into opportunity — working to close the nation's literacy gap by providing literacy tutoring scholarships to first-grade students reading below grade level. We are a Scholarship Granting Organization (SGO) leveraging the new $1,700 federal Educational Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC), effective January 1, 2027, to build the most ambitious literacy funding campaign in the country.

The Role
The Contract Recruiter will lead full-cycle recruiting for EducationSuperHighway's first major hiring push across six to eight roles spanning partnerships, marketing, program operations, and organizational support. This is a high-volume, fast-moving engagement for a recruiter who is comfortable working independently, managing multiple searches simultaneously, and closing strong candidates quickly.

This is a six-month contract with potential to extend based on organizational need.

Key Responsibilities

Full Lifecycle Recruiting

 - Manage full-cycle recruiting for multiple open roles simultaneously, from job posting through offer acceptance.

 - Source candidates through job boards, LinkedIn, networks, and referrals, with a strong bias toward active outreach over inbound-only pipelines.

 - Screen candidates, coordinate interviews, and manage candidate communications throughout the process, ensuring a timely and positive candidate experience.

 - Conduct reference checks and coordinate background screenings on final candidates.

 - Draft and extend offers in coordination with leadership.

 - Support job description development for roles that are not yet fully defined.

Search Management and Coordination

 - Partner closely with ESH leadership to understand role requirements, priorities, and ideal candidate profiles.

 - Provide regular updates and reports on pipeline status, interview feedback, and search progress for each open role.

 - Advise on compensation benchmarking and sourcing strategy as needed.

 - Coordinate with ESH leadership on offer logistics and onboarding handoff.

 - Build and maintain a referral pipeline by working ESH's leadership network and encouraging warm introductions.

Tools and Reporting

 - Manage candidate pipelines, interview notes, and search status in Ashby, ESH's ATS, keeping all records accurate and up to date.

 - Produce a regular pipeline report for leadership covering open requisitions, candidate status, and projected time-to-fill.

 - Advise leadership on Ashby configuration and workflow setup as needed to support ESH's first major hiring push.

Qualifications

Required

 - 5+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience, including significant time working as an independent contractor or agency recruiter managing multiple searches.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage six or more concurrent searches without losing momentum.

 - Strong sourcing skills, including active outreach via LinkedIn Recruiter and other channels.

 - Experience setting up or administering an ATS (Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, or similar).

 - Excellent communication and relationship management skills with both candidates and hiring managers.

 - Comfort working in a startup or early-stage environment where priorities can shift quickly.

Highly Valued

 - Experience recruiting for nonprofit, education, or mission-driven organizations.

 - Familiarity with partnership, business development, or sales recruiting.

 - Experience using recruiter networks, or similar platforms for contract engagements.

 - Track record of closing candidates for roles that require mission alignment, not just compensation.



EducationSup

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Employment Type: Contract | Potential to Convert to Full-Time

Location: Remote

About EducationSuperHighway

EducationSuperHighway, the national non-profit that connected 99.7% of American students to high-speed internet and 5 million low-income households to affordable broadband, is now launching its 3.0 mission to end the reading crisis in America's K-12 schools.

Beginning in 2027, the federal Education Freedom Tax Credit (EFTC) creates a new mechanism for employers to offer education philanthropy as a workplace benefit. ESH is building the infrastructure that makes this real, connecting employers, employees, and payroll systems into a seamless giving experience.

This is ESH's first engineering hire for this product. You will make real architecture decisions, own third-party integrations, and build the platform that brings this benefit to life for employers and employees nationwide ahead of the 2027 launch. This engagement starts as a contract role with a strong path to full-time conversion for the right person.

What You Will Do

• Design and build an API-first full-stack platform connecting employers, employees, and payroll systems

• Own integrations with payroll and HRIS providers — authorization flows, data sync, and deduction management

• Build employee-facing enrollment flows and employer-facing campaign and dashboard tooling

• Design reliable background job pipelines with status tracking, retry logic, and error handling

• Handle sensitive employee and financial data with appropriate security practices

• Make sound architecture decisions under ambiguity, balancing speed with long-term extensibility

 

What We Are Looking For

Required

 

• 7+ years of full-stack engineering experience building production web applications

• Early-stage startup experience, working with ambiguity, and building from 0 to 1.

• Demonstrated use of AI coding tools such as Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or Claude — as a regular part of your development workflow, including planning, code generation, debugging, and refactoring

• Mature judgment about AI outputs: knowing when to trust, when to correct, and when to set the tools aside

• Strong third-party API integration experience, particularly OAuth flows and structured data write-back

• Experience modeling relational data for complex, multi-step stateful workflows

• Proven ability to build reliable background job pipelines with operational visibility

• Secure handling of PII and financial data

• Clear communication and sound judgment in early-stage, ambiguous environments

 

Highly Valued

• Experience integrating with payroll or HRIS platforms — UKG, Rippling, ADP, Workday, or similar

• Background in HR tech, benefits, workplace giving, or fintech

• Familiarity with payroll deduction mechanics or year-end tax document workflows

 

Compensation & Benefits

EducationSuperHighway offers market-rate compensation competitive with for-profit companies. Specific compensation details will be shared with candidates during the interview process.

For candidates who convert to full-time, ESH offers a comprehensive benefits package including:

• Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits

• Generous PTO and flexible remote work

• The opportunity to build a team and function from scratch and to help close the K-12 literacy gap in America

notes: Non-profit with a small tech presence
